name: CosmoLex Legal Practice Management API
what_it_does: CosmoLex REST API for integrated legal practice management and trust accounting for small and solo law firms. Enables AI agents to manage matter and case lifecycle management for law firm practice automation, handle integrated trust accounting and IOLTA compliance without QuickBooks dependency, access time and expense tracking for attorney billing management, retrieve legal billing and invoice generation for revenue management, manage document management and organization for legal workflow, handle client portal and communication management for client service, access docket and deadline tracking for case calendar management, retrieve trust and operating account reconciliation for legal financial compliance, manage matter-based financial management and reporting, and integrate CosmoLex data with legal research, payment, and document management platforms.
use_cases: Managing matters from legal practice automation agents
Handling trust accounting from legal financial agents
Generating bills from legal billing agents
Tracking deadlines from docket management agents
not_for: Large law firms requiring enterprise-grade legal practice management
Corporate legal departments without law firm trust accounting needs
Non-legal businesses without attorney billing and trust context
best_when: A solo or small law firm using CosmoLex wants AI agents to automate matter management, trust accounting, time tracking, and billing without relying on QuickBooks integration.
avoid_when: LEGAL ETHICS RISK: Trust accounting automation must comply with state bar IOLTA rules — every state has specific trust accounting requirements for three-way reconciliation; automated trust operations without bar rule compliance creates disciplinary and financial risk. Attorney-client privileged matter data requires confidentiality; automated data sharing must respect privilege. CosmoLex API documentation is limited compared to market leaders (Clio); integration may require vendor engagement.

auth.notes: CosmoLex uses OAuth 2.0. API documentation at developer.cosmolex.com. No native webhooks. REST API with JSON. Salt Lake City, Utah HQ. AbacusNext subsidiary (acquired 2019). AbacusNext is PE-backed. Founded 2013. 5,000+ law firm customers. Solo and small law firm focus. Strong in trust accounting integration. Competes with Clio, MyCase, and Smokeball for small law firm practice management.

pricing.notes: Salt Lake City. CosmoLex (AbacusNext). Founded 2013. 5,000+ law firm customers. Solo/small firm focus. Trust accounting without QuickBooks differentiator. Competes with Clio and MyCase for small law firm software.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per tier

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL ETHICS RISK: Trust accounting IOLTA compliance varies by state bar; automated trust fund movements without state bar rule validation creates disciplinary risk

Three-way trust reconciliation — bar rules require periodic three-way reconciliation of trust ledger, bank statement, and individual client ledgers; automated reconciliation must maintain complete audit trail
No webhooks — event-driven legal practice automation requires polling for matter and billing status changes
Limited API documentation — CosmoLex API documentation less comprehensive than market leaders; integration may require vendor professional services

Attorney billing review — automated bill generation from time entries requires attorney review for billing judgment (e.g., block billing ethics, reasonable fee); automated bills without review risk bar complaints
AbacusNext ownership — CosmoLex owned by AbacusNext PE; product direction and API investment may be limited vs. independent VC-backed competitors
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal PM and trust accounting. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Attorney-client privileged and trust account data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal PM. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Legal matter and trust account data.
